_meta.purpose: Permanent source of truth for all approved agency deliverables. Every approved asset must be recorded here. This file is the custodian — not the human.
_meta.instructions: When an asset is approved: add an entry here immediately, commit to git, push. When starting a new session: read this file first before asking any human where anything is.

houzz_gallery.name: Houzz Portfolio Gallery
houzz_gallery.description: 20 high-quality AI-generated interior/exterior remodeling images for Renova Builders Houzz profile. Square format (1080x1080). Renova logo in bottom corner with horizontal rectangular dark banner.

brand_standards.logo_background: horizontal rectangle, semi-transparent dark, elegant minimal premium — NOT a square
brand_standards.style: photorealistic, luxury, high-end renovation, editorial quality

approved_template.note: Template established in prior sessions. Kitchen image with horizontal rectangular Renova logo banner in bottom-left corner is the approved direction. All subsequent images must match this standard exactly.
approved_template.generation_id: TO_BE_CONFIRMED — user must confirm which generation is the locked template

asset_lifecycle: 1. GENERATE — asset created in Higgsfield or other tool
2. LOG — immediately add entry to this registry with status: pending_approval
3. SUBMIT — present to approver with registry ID
4. DECISION — approved: update status + approval_date + approved_by; rejected: update status + notes
5. CATALOG — approved assets committed to git immediately
6. DELIVER — delivered assets flagged as delivered with delivery date and recipient
7. REUSE — future sessions check registry before generating anything new
session_startup_protocol: 2. Check approved assets by client and project
3. Continue from last approved state — do not regenerate approved work
4. Do not ask the human where assets are — the registry is the answer
